toc: Veröffentlichte Werke mit Opuszahl. Kirchen-Musik, op. 23. Nr. 1. Choral Aus tiefer Not : für Solostimmen, gemischten Chor und Orgel, MWV B 20 ; Nr. 2. title: Songs and arias ornamented by Giovanni Battista Velluti, the last operatic castrato
author: Velluti, Giovanni Battista; Crowe, Robert (Singer)
abstract: "This edition consists of twenty-nine songs and arias from the early nineteenth-century solo vocal music repertoire to which Giovanni Battista (Giambattista) Velluti (1780-1861) composed (and frequently, though not always, published) ornamentation. The last great operatic castrato, Velluti, over the course of his career, became increasingly well known not only for his singing, but also for his creative, adventuresome ornaments. His publications of ornamented songs and arias were frequent throughout the last decade of his career, when his singing style became less purely virtuosic and more concerned with affect. The edition includes only ornamented solo songs and arias with a secure attribution to Velluti. Those with doubtful or spurious attribution are excluded, as are Velluti's ornamented duets and trios, as well as arias in full orchestral score." --
toc: Otto variazioni sul tema "Nel cor più non mi sento" ; Or che la notte placida ; Quel tuo girar del ciglio / Giovanni Battista Velluti -- L'amor timido ; Placido zeffiretto ; Compagni, amici, addio -- Deh non soffrir ; Amor soave ; Gentil usignolo / John Fane, Lord Burghersh -- Cara, nel dir che t'amo / Pietro Ceracchini -- Ah! che per me non v'è / Johann Simon Mayr -- Soave immagine / Saverio Mercadante -- Notte tremenda -- Caro suono lusinghier / Francesco Morlacchi -- Ecco, o numi -- Ah! quando cesserà ; Nere funeste immagini ; Dite al mio bene ; Or che la luna ; Or che l'indissolubile / Giuseppe Nicolini -- I fieri palpiti / Giovanni Pacini -- Dolce de' bardi il canto / Stefano Pavesi -- La tua diletta immagine / ?Pavesi -- Il canto ; Der Tag ist verschwunden / Giovanni Battista Perucchini -- T'abbraccio, ti stringo / Gioachino Rossini -- Api erranti / Nicola Vaccaj -- Come potrei mai vivere / Joseph Weigl -- Appendix 1. A can ai think of deis gone bai ; Ah, can I think of days gone by / Thomas Welsh -- Appendix 2. La rimembranza ; Il desiderio / Giovanni Battista Perucchini.
notes: edited by Robert Crowe.; Chiefly for medium voice and piano; 9th work for medium voice and continuo, 22nd work for medium voice and guitar.; Appendices contain two versions of a song by Thomas Welsh, ornamented by Velluti and two ariette by Giovanni Battista Perucchini, possibly ornamented by Velluti.; Introduction and critical report in English.; Includes bibliographical references.; Chiefly Italian words, in part in German and English; also printed separately as text with English translations.; Staff notation.

title: 45 songs on poems of Goethe and Eichendorff : for voice and piano
author: Wolf, Hugo; Goethe, Johann Wolfgang von; Eichendorff, Joseph Freiherr von; Appelbaum, Stanley
abstract: German words; Eichendorff-Lieder include English translations. English translations of Goethe-Lieder printed as texts (p. xiii-xix). Reprinted from undated editions published by C.F. Peters, Leipzig. With new headings and added features including a German-English contents, title-first line indexes, and new translations of the Goethe texts by Stanley Appelbaum. Contains Gedichte von J. von Eichendorff and no. 12-18 and 34-51 of Gedichte von Goethe.
toc: Eichendorff songs, Nos. 1-20 (complete). Der Freund [The friend] ; Der Musikant [The wandering minstrel] ; Verschwiegene Liebe [Silent love] ; Das Ständchen [The aged minstrel] ; Der Soldat I [The soldier, I] ; Der Soldat II [The soldier, II] ; Die Zigeunerin [The Gipsy-maid]; Nachtzauber [Night's glory] ; Der Schreckenberger [Captain Dreadnaught] ; Der Glücksritter [Dame Fortune's knight] ; Lieber alles [I'd rather] ; Heimweh [Longing for home] ; Der Scholar [The itinerant scholar] ; Der verzweifelte Liebhaber [The despairing lover] ; Unfall [Mishap] ; Liebesglück [The bliss of love] ; Seemanns Abschied [The sailor's farewell] ; Erwartung [Waiting] ; Die Nacht [Night] ; Waldmädchen [Forest-nymph] -- Goethe songs, Nos. 12-18, Nos. 34-51. Ritter Kurts Brautfahrt [Sir Kurt's bridal journey] ; Gutmann und Gutweib [Goodman and Goodwife] ; Cophtisches Lied I [Song of the Great Copt, I]; Cophtisches Lied II [Song of the Great Copt, II] ; Frech und froh I [Impudent and merry, I] ; Frech und froh II [Impudent and merry, II]. Beherzigung [A reflection] ; Ob der Koran von Ewigkeit sei? [Is the Koran eternal] ; Trunken müssen wir alle sein! [We must be drunk!] ; So lang man nüchtern ist [As long as a man is sober] ; Sie haben wegen der Trunkenheit [They have complained about our drunkenness] ; Was in der Schenke waren heute [What commotions there were in the inn today] ; Nicht Gelegenheit macht Diebe [It is not opportunity that makes the thief] ; Hoch beglückt in deiner Liebe [Deeply blessed by your love] ; Als ich auf dem Euphrat schiffte [As I was boating on the Euphrates] ; Dies zu deuten bin erbötig! [I am ready to interpret this!] ; Hätt ich irgend wohl Bedenken [Would I have any reservations?] ; Komm, Liebchen, komm! [Come, darling come!] ; Wie sollt ich heiter bleiben [How am I to remain cheerful?] ; Wenn ich dein gedenke [When I think of you] ; Locken, haltet mich gefangen [Tresses, keep me captive] ; Nimmer will ich dich verlieren! [I never want to lose you!]; Prometheus [Prometheus] ; Ganymed [Ganymede] ; Grenzen der Menschheit [Limitations of humanity].
notes: Hugo Wolf.; Compilation of songs originally published by C.F. Peters, Leipzig, [n.d.]; Prose translations of Goethe's German texts (xiii-xix) by Stanley Appelbaum.; Includes glossary of German terms in the music (xi-xii).; Eichendorff songs with German/English underlays.

title: 45 arias from operas and oratorios
author: Handel, George Frideric; Kagen, Sergius
abstract: Italian and English words; the Italian arias also printed as text with English translations preceding each aria. Pl. no.: 1694, 1696, 1698. v. 2 added, entry closed. vk02 03-28-90
toc: Volume 1. Serse. Và godendo -- L'allegro. Come and trip it -- Rinaldo. Cara sposa -- Semele. Where'er you walk -- The passion. Chi sprezzando / text by B.H. Brockes -- Siroe. Ch'io mai vi possa -- Rinaldo. Lascia ch'io pianga -- Amadigi. Ah! spietato! -- Radamisto. Ombra cara -- Partenope. Furibondo spira il vento -- Scipione. Recit. Nulla temer ; Aria, Generoso chi sol brama / piano reduction by Ernst Hess -- Tolomeo. Stille amare -- Judas Maccabeus. Father of Heav'n -- Joshua. See the raging flames -- Acis and Galatea. Recit, I rage, i melt, I burn! ; Aria, O ruddier than the cherry. Volume 2. Atalanta. Care selve -- Radamisto. Sommi Dei -- Semele. O sleep, why dost thou leave me? -- Tolomeo. Non lo dirò col labbro -- Sosarme. Recit. Rasserena, o madre ; Aria, Rend'il sereno al ciglio -- Serse. Recit. Frondi tenere ; Aria, Ombra mai fù -- Dettinger Te Deum. Vouchsafe, O Lord -- Alcina. Verdi prati -- Ottone. Ah! tu non sai -- Samson. How willing my paternal love -- Floridante. Finchè lo strale -- Judas Maccabeus. Recit. I feel the deity within ; Aria, Arm, arm, ye brave! -- Admeto. Cangiò d'aspetto -- Berenice. Sì, tra i ceppi -- Alexander's feast. Revenge, Timotheus cries -- Scipione. Tutta raccolta ancor. Volume 3. Floridante. Alma mia -- All thanks to Thee, Lord. Dank sei Dir, Herr / [spuriosly attributed to Handel] ; arranged and edited by Siegfried Ochs ; English version by Humphrey Procter-Gregg -- Ottone. Affanni del pensier ; Del minacciar del vento -- Giulio Cesare. Recit. E pur così in un giorno ; Aria, Piangerò la sorte mia -- Orlando. Sorge infausta una procella -- Samson. Return, oh God of hosts! ; Honor and arms ; Thy glorious deeds inspired my tongue -- Rodelinda. Con rauco mormorio ; Dove sei, amato bene? -- Amadigi. Recit. D'un sventurato amante ; Aria, Pena tiranna io sento -- Ezio. Nasce al bosco -- Alexander's feast. Bacchus ever fair and young.
notes: Handel ; selected and edited by Sergius Kagen ; English translations by Nicholas Granitto and Waldo Lyman ; for voice and piano.; Italian, English and German words; the Italian arias also printed as text with English translations preceding each aria.; Pref. in English.; Pl. no.: 1694, 1696, 1698.

title: Verses for ensembles ; Nenia : the death of Orpheus ; The fields of sorrow
author: Birtwistle, Harrison; Ausonius, Decimus Magnus; Manning, Jane; Atherton, David; Hacker, Alan; Zinovieff, Peter; Matrix (Rock group); London Sinfonietta
abstract: The 1st work for 2 sopranos, chorus and 16 players; the 2nd work for 5 woodwinds, 5 brass, and 3 percussionists; the 3rd work a 'dramatic scene' for soprano, 3 bass clarinets, clarinet, piano, prepared piano, and crotales. The 1st work sung in Latin; the 3rd work sung in English. Text of the 1st work by Decimus Ausonius ; text of the 3rd work by Peter Zinovieff. Jane Manning, soprano (1st, 3rd works) ; London Sinfonietta, David Atherton, conductor ; The Matrix, Alan Hacker, conductor. Recorded in January & May 1973, London. "Lyrita recorded edition"--Container. Compact disc. Program notes and texts in English inserted in container. Durations: 9:58 ; 28:13 ; 17:45.
notes: Harrison Birtwistle.; The 1st work for piccolo, oboe, E♭ clarinet, B♭ clarinet, bassoon (1st group); alto flute, English horn, B♭ clarinet, bass clarinet, contrabassoon (2nd group); 2 trumpets, horn, 2 trombones (3rd group); and percussion (3 players). The score indicates that the players of the higher pitched woodwinds double on the lower pitched instruments. The 2nd work for narrator and soprano (1 vocalist), crotales, 2 pianos (1 prepared), and 3 clarinets. The 3rd work for 3 flutes, 3 English horns, 3 bass clarinets, 3 bassoons, horn, vibraphone, 2 pianos, 2 sopranos, and mixed chorus (SSAATTBB).; 2nd work sung in English, 3rd in Latin.; Text of 2nd work by Peter Zinovieff, of the 3rd from the Aeniad by Ausonius.; Program notes by M. Nyman and biographical notes on the composer in English, French, and German, text of the 2nd work with French and German translations, and text of the 3rd work with English, French, and German translations (7 p. : ports.) laid in container.; Jane Manning, soprano (2nd and 3rd works) ; London Sinfonietta, David Atherton, conductor (1st and 3rd works); undentified chorus (3rd work); Matrix, Alan Hacker, director (2nd work).; "Recorded in Kingsway Hall, London, in January and May 1973."

title: Dark city
author: Proyas, Alex,; Mason, Andrew.; Dobbs, Lem; Goyer, David S.; Sewell, Rufus,; Sutherland, Kiefer,; Connelly, Jennifer; Hurt, William; O'Brien, Richard,; Richardson, Ian,; New Line Cinema Corporation; Mystery Clock Productions (Firm); New Line Home Video (Firm)
abstract: "When John Murdoch awakens in a strange hotel room, he finds that he is wanted for a series of brutal murders. The problem is he can't remember a thing. Pursued by the police and haunted by the Strangers, mysterious beings who possess the ability to stop time and alter reality, he seeks to unravel the twisted riddle of his identity. But in a city where reality is the ultimate illusion, discovering the truth could be fatal."--Container.
toc: Widescreen format (aspect ratio 2.35:1) on side A -- Full screen format on side B.
notes: New Line Cinema ; a Mystery Clock production ; directed by Alex Proyas ; screenplay by Alex Proyas and Lem Dobbs and David S. Goyer ; story by Alex Proyas ; produced by Andrew Mason and Alex Proyas ; an Alex Proyas film.; DVD.; English or French language soundtrack (5.1 surround) with subtitles available in English, French and Spanish ; closed-captioned in English for the hearing impaired.; Rufus Sewell, Kiefer Sutherland, Jennifer Connelly, Richard O'Brien, Ian Richardson, William Hurt, Colin Friels, Mitchell Butel, Frank Gallacher, Bruce Spence, Melissa George, John Bluthal.; Director of photography, Dariusz Wolski ; editor, Dov Hoenig ; music, Trevor Jones.; Widescreen format (aspect ratio 2.35:1) on side A, full screen format on side B.; Originally released as a motion picture.; Special features include: Audio commentary by Roger Ebert ; audio commentary by director, writers, director of photography and production designer ; Find shell beach, interactive game ; cast & crew biographies, filmographies, and highlights ; comparisons to Fritz Lang's Metropolis ; theatrical trailer ; set design ; animated menus ; etc.
